lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Bay Four. The secondary patient — a woman, mid-fifties, restrained by instinct more than injury — is not the critical. She is not the one anyone is watching. That is the problem.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: I need a pressure read. Now. Come on.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Ninety over sixty. She's stable, Reza.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: She doesn't look stable.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: She looks scared. Those are different things.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza's hands are on the patient's wrist. Not taking a pulse. Just — there. Still. He has been still for four seconds longer than four seconds allows in this room.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: There's something in the spine. I can feel it.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Nothing flagged on intake—

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: I know what flagged. I'm telling you what I feel.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo watches him. Reza Shirazi has been back six weeks. Six weeks since the inquiry closed, since the board said the word 'reinstated' in the tone they reserve for things that are technically true. Six weeks of Reza doing everything exactly right, in the way that people do when they are terrified of doing anything wrong.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Okay. Walk me through it.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: She came in braced. Both arms up. Passenger side impact, but she was braced like she saw it coming.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: Anticipatory extension.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: C5 and C6. I want a scan.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: You'll have it. Order it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A beat. Reza orders it. His voice does not shake. Theo does not make a thing of that.

lines.speaker: Sister Agnes
lines.text: Doctor Shirazi.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She appears at the edge of bay four the way Sister Agnes always appears — not arriving so much as having been present slightly longer than anyone noticed. She is holding nothing. She requires nothing. She is simply there, in the particular way that suggests she has already seen what she came to see.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: Sister.

lines.speaker: Sister Agnes
lines.text: The woman's daughter is in the family room. She's been told nothing yet.

lines.speaker: Reza
lines.text: We'll update when we have the scan.

lines.speaker: Sister Agnes
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She does not leave immediately. She looks at Reza's hands, which are now moving again, competent and certain, ordering the scan, calling the tech, doing the ten things at once that this room demands. And then she looks at Theo, who is watching Reza with the quiet, careful attention of a man standing at the base of something tall and uncertain of its load.

lines.speaker: Sister Agnes
lines.text: He ordered the right thing.

lines.speaker: Theo
lines.text: He usually does.

lines.speaker: Sister Agnes
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She crosses the bay. Does not look toward bay one, where Maeve and Dana are still working, where the silence between two people has its own atmospheric pressure. She does not look because she has already seen that too. Sister Agnes has been in this building for nineteen years. She knows what it looks like when the past walks back through the ambulance doors. She knows what it looks like when someone is being held together by the work itself, by the specific mercy of having something necessary to do with their hands. She knows both of those things are happening tonight, in this room, at the same time, and that neither of them is her business to name. Only to see.
